hero.title: Farever
hero.description: Farever is an online multiplayer action RPG set in a vibrant fantasy open world. Explore Siagarta, clear dungeons, craft powerful gear, build your own class style, and follow the Early Access roadmap.

gameFeature.title: Farever Gameplay
gameFeature.description: Explore Siagarta, combine class skills with weapons, clear dungeons with friends, and shape your progression through jobs, crafting, and gear upgrades.

lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.title: Farever Early Access Release and Download
lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.intro: Farever is live on Steam Early Access, and this section gives the fastest launch snapshot for new and returning players.

steps.title: Check the current release status
steps.description: The game is available now in Steam Early Access for PC, with active live-service updates during the access period.

steps.title: Review launch pricing by region
steps.description: Pricing varies by currency region. Check your Steam storefront to confirm the current local price and launch promotions.

steps.title: Confirm platform and account setup
steps.description: A Steam account and PC installation are required. Keep your client updated to avoid matchmaking and version mismatch issues.

steps.title: Pick the closest server region
steps.description: Choosing the nearest region reduces latency, improves combat responsiveness, and stabilizes party play in instanced content.

quickTips: Use the Steam store page as the source of truth for price and package updates.
If a launch offer is active, buy within the visible countdown window in your region.
Coordinate server region with friends before creating your first party session.
Keep graphics drivers current before first launch to avoid shader compilation issues.

lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.title: Farever Beginner Guide
lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.intro: Early progress in Farever is smoother when you follow a simple loop: explore, fight, gather, craft, and then push into harder content.

cards.name: Start with map awareness
cards.description: Learn travel routes, safe recovery spots, and fast return paths before you chain difficult encounters.

cards.name: Commit to one class first
cards.description: Focus one class and one role in your first hours so your gear and skill decisions stay efficient.

cards.name: Rotate activities for materials
cards.description: Alternate open-world events, dungeon runs, and gathering routes to keep both combat and crafting progress moving.

cards.name: Upgrade in short cycles
cards.description: Apply small upgrades frequently instead of waiting for perfect drops. Consistent gains matter more than rare spikes.

milestones: Complete your first world activity chain
Craft your first meaningful gear upgrade

lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.title: Farever Classes Guide
lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.intro: Farever launches with four core classes. Choose based on role responsibility, positioning comfort, and the pace you enjoy in combat.

items.name: Warrior
items.description: Durable melee control with steady pressure. Great for players who want direct engagement and stable survivability.

items.name: Cleric
items.description: Team utility and sustain focus. Strong in group content where timing and support rotations shape fight consistency.

items.name: Mage
items.description: Spell-driven damage from range. Rewards clean spacing and cooldown sequencing for safe, high-impact output.

items.name: Rogue
items.description: Fast melee burst with high movement demands. Best for aggressive players who can manage risk windows.

items.name: Solo class priority
items.description: Warrior is the easiest starting point for solo progression due to forgiving frontline play and smooth early stability.

items.name: Party class priority
items.description: Cleric and Warrior form the most reliable beginner duo foundation before scaling into specialized team compositions.

lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.title: Farever Best Class and Role Picks
lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.intro: Pick classes by objective instead of chasing a universal tier list. The best option changes with your role and group context.

solutions.name: Best beginner baseline
solutions.description: Strong survivability and straightforward combat flow make Warrior the most stable first pick for new players.

solutions.name: Best co-op anchor
solutions.description: Cleric raises team consistency through sustain and support utility, especially in extended dungeon encounters.

solutions.name: Best ranged pressure
solutions.description: Mage excels when your team needs safe damage uptime and strong ranged burst during boss mechanics.

solutions.name: Best high-tempo melee
solutions.description: Rogue is ideal for players comfortable with mobility-intensive execution and aggressive timing windows.

managementTips: For solo starts, prioritize durability and recoverability before peak damage.
For duos, pair frontline stability with support coverage for smoother clears.
For full parties, diversify roles to avoid overlapping cooldown gaps.
Re-evaluate your role after each major gear milestone rather than every run.

lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.title: Farever Builds and Playstyles
lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.intro: Build strength in Farever comes from class identity, weapon synergy, skill sequencing, and stat discipline working together.

cards.name: Bruiser control builds
cards.description: Frontline setups that trade burst for uptime, using mitigation and sustained pressure to hold space in group fights.

cards.name: Ranged burst builds
cards.description: High-output casting patterns focused on cooldown timing, spacing, and safe windows during boss phases.

cards.name: Mobility pressure builds
cards.description: Fast melee patterns that chain movement skills into burst opportunities while avoiding overcommitment.

cards.name: Support utility builds
cards.description: Team-first configurations that stabilize encounters through sustain, mitigation, and utility skill cadence.

highlights: Match weapon families to your role goal
Stack stats that reinforce your combat loop
Use defensive tools before overloading damage slots
Refine one build path at a time for clear upgrades

lucidBlocksWorldRegions.title: Farever Weapons and Arsenal Guide
lucidBlocksWorldRegions.intro: Weapon choice changes how each class feels. Focus on handling profile, skill synergy, and encounter type when building an arsenal.

regions.name: Close-range weapon sets
regions.description: Best for sustained pressure and control in melee lanes. Pair with durability and recovery-focused stats.

regions.name: Ranged catalyst sets
regions.description: Supports spacing-heavy combat with strong burst windows and flexible utility between cooldown cycles.

regions.name: High-tempo duel sets
regions.description: Fast execution profile for players who prefer short commit windows and active positional control.

regions.name: Hybrid support sets
regions.description: Balanced loadouts that maintain utility uptime while preserving enough personal damage to contribute.

lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.title: Farever Combat Guide
lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.intro: Winning harder encounters is less about raw stats and more about timing discipline, positioning, and coordinated cooldown use.

creatures.name: Open with spacing checks
creatures.description: Start each pull by reading enemy reach and movement speed before committing high-cooldown skills.

creatures.name: Dodge before burst
creatures.description: Avoid avoidable damage first, then convert recovery windows into safe burst cycles.

creatures.name: Preserve one emergency tool
creatures.description: Keep one defensive cooldown or mobility option for unexpected mechanics and late-phase pressure spikes.

creatures.name: Respect boss phase shifts
creatures.description: When phase behavior changes, reset spacing and re-establish role positioning before re-engaging.

creatures.name: Coordinate party roles
creatures.description: Call defensive rotations and target priority to avoid overlapping mistakes in multi-wave fights.

creatures.name: Track stamina and cooldown rhythm
creatures.description: Stable clears come from rhythm control, not constant aggression. Plan tempo around reliable resource windows.

lucidBlocksMobilityGear.title: Farever Map and Exploration Guide
lucidBlocksMobilityGear.intro: Exploration efficiency in Siagarta depends on route planning, vertical traversal awareness, and consistent return loops for upgrades.

items.name: Primary region loops
items.description: Build repeatable loops between activities, gathering points, and recovery hubs to reduce idle travel time.

items.name: Landmark-based navigation
items.description: Use distinct terrain markers and settlement anchors to keep route memory reliable across long sessions.

items.name: Vertical pathing
items.description: Prioritize movement tools that shorten climbs and descents, especially near event-dense terrain.

items.name: Cave and side-path sweeps
items.description: Secondary paths frequently contain valuable materials and shortcut opportunities for future runs.

items.name: Underwater and hazard routing
items.description: Approach hazard zones with planned entry and exit points so failed attempts do not waste progression time.

items.name: Session-end consolidation
items.description: Finish each route near a crafting or storage hub to immediately convert gathered resources into progression.

unlockMilestones: Establish one fast-return route to core hubs
Integrate side-path farming into regular travel
End runs with direct upgrade conversion

lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.title: Farever Dungeons, Instances, and Bosses
lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.intro: Farever Early Access lists ten instance encounters split into five dungeons and five boss fights. Use this tracker to plan clear order, assign party jobs, and prepare for higher-difficulty modes when they launch.

sections.name: Nost Mire · Dungeon
sections.description: Run a repeatable combat route with pack control, resource checks, and a boss finish that supports steady gear progression.

sections.name: The Abyss · Dungeon
sections.description: Focus on fixed encounter pacing instead of open-world roaming to practice rotations and coordination under pressure.

sections.name: The Hivetree · Dungeon
sections.description: Push through bee-region encounters to progress linked materials, instance rewards, and boss readiness.

sections.name: The Kobold Mine · Dungeon
sections.description: Farm kobold-focused routes for cave progression items and dungeon reward loops that feed crafting jobs.

sections.name: The Sunken City · Dungeon
sections.description: Clear underwater ruin objectives for a different encounter rhythm and expanded route variety beyond open-world camps.

sections.name: Carbygenus · Boss
sections.description: Use this encounter for pattern learning, burst-window discipline, and rare reward targeting with coordinated roles.

sections.name: Honeysabath · Boss
sections.description: Track clears to stabilize bee-region progression and validate survivability setups before harder content.

sections.name: Monster Chuck · Boss
sections.description: Treat this fight as a mechanical step-up where timing and sustained execution matter more than one-shot attempts.

sections.name: Mukshi · Boss
sections.description: Keep this boss in your progression sheet as a milestone encounter for build consistency and team rhythm.

sections.name: Ratzar · Boss
sections.description: Prioritize this clear for kobold-route progression and boss-specific drops tied to weapon and glider goals.

sections.name: Heroic and Challenge Modes · Difficulty Roadmap
sections.description: Plan long-term build upgrades around future difficulty tiers so current farming choices still pay off later.

growthMilestones: Clear at least two dungeons with assigned role responsibilities
Record first-kill notes for all five listed boss encounters
Convert instance drops into one measurable build upgrade path
Prepare a loadout plan for Heroic and Challenge mode entry

lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.title: Farever Loot Sources and Drop Tables
lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.intro: Use source-first farming routes when you already know the item you want. This table summary maps monsters, bosses, gathering nodes, and vault rewards so loot runs stay efficient and repeatable.

priorities.name: AncientThyme and Ore_Copper Gathering
priorities.description: Gatherables supply stable baseline materials: AncientThymePetal and CopperOre at 100%, plus SparkSample around 25% for early crafting loops.

priorities.name: Bee and Boar Monster Routes
priorities.description: Bee and Boar routes provide high-volume crafting drops with rare mounts at 0.10%, supporting both progression and collection goals in one loop.

priorities.name: Crab, Kobold, and Manfish Material Pools
priorities.description: Farm seafood, fang, scale, and glider materials by target family so recipe ingredients and mobility collection stay aligned.

priorities.name: Crabgantua Boss Drops (lv 8)
priorities.description: Target this boss for 1.0% weapon and mount rewards, including Shield_OrbitWater and Mount_Crab_Blue.

priorities.name: Nepsilon Boss Drops (lv 5)
priorities.description: Run Nepsilon for early 1.0% boss rewards such as Halos_Totem, DA_Water, and Glider_FlyingFish_Orange.

priorities.name: King Ratsar Boss Drops (lv 10)
priorities.description: Farm for Daggers_DuplicatePoison, GA_Craft, and Glider_Bat_WhiteBlack to support rogue builds and collectible goals.

priorities.name: Vault_Z1_1 Guaranteed Reward
priorities.description: Track this vault for guaranteed Gold and Glider_FlyingFish_Red when you need predictable progression payout.

priorities.name: Vault_Z1_2 Guaranteed Reward
priorities.description: Use this vault route for guaranteed Gold and Mount_Crab_Yellow to lock in collection progress without RNG variance.

lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.title: Farever Crafting Jobs and Recipes
lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.intro: Farever recipe progression is centered on six professions. Compare each job by role, recipe count, and early-value crafts so your material farming feeds the right build upgrades.

achievements.title: Main role
achievements.description: Metal gear, ingots, plates, and weapon/armor support for frontline progression.

achievements.title: Early value
achievements.description: Strong first pick for Warrior-style paths and durability-focused upgrades.

achievements.title: Main role
achievements.description: Cloth and leather gear, bags, back pieces, and soft armor support lanes.

achievements.title: Early value
achievements.description: Reliable for bag upgrades and light-armor users who need faster inventory scaling.

achievements.title: Example crafts
achievements.description: Bag_Z2 (lv 3), Back_RCrimson_AssCle_Craft (lv 5), and Feet gear crafts at lv 1-3.

achievements.title: Main role
achievements.description: Gems, rings, pendants, prospecting, and stat-tuning upgrades for precision builds.

achievements.title: Early value
achievements.description: Best for players prioritizing jewelry slots and ore-to-gem conversion value.

achievements.title: Main role
achievements.description: Elixirs, potions, and utility buffs supporting strength, dexterity, healing, and movement.

achievements.title: Early value
achievements.description: High-impact support profession for dungeon attempts and boss preparation windows.

achievements.title: Main role
achievements.description: Food buffs and practical dishes built from common monster and fishing materials.

achievements.title: Early value
achievements.description: Excellent starter profession for repeatable sustain buffs during leveling routes.

achievements.title: Example crafts
achievements.description: Crab Goulash, Wild Boar Stew, Grilled Mackerel, and Plainswalker Feast.

achievements.title: Main role
achievements.description: Enchant papers, powders, formulas, and scroll support for gear scaling.

achievements.title: Early value
achievements.description: Strong for players who want stat formulas and later weapon enchant depth.

achievements.title: Example crafts
achievements.description: EnchantPaper_Z1, EnchantPowder_Z1, EnchantConcentrate_Z1, and Magic Formula: Strength.

lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.title: Farever Gear Items and Rarity Guide
lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.intro: Use category, rarity, class, slot, and level filters to decide what to equip, craft, sell, or keep. Farever item coverage includes weapons, armor, consumables, components, gems, mounts, gliders, and upgrade currencies.

faqs.question: How should I filter Farever weapons for quick upgrades?
faqs.answer: Filter by class, level bracket, and rarity first, then choose weapon families that match your skill rotations and role pressure.

faqs.question: What is the best way to evaluate armor and gear slots?
faqs.answer: Compare slot relevance and stat direction before rarity color so each replacement improves your active build instead of just item score.

faqs.question: Which consumables are worth carrying into dungeons?
faqs.answer: Keep healing, movement, and primary-stat consumables ready so attempts stay stable during long pulls and boss mechanics.

faqs.question: How should I handle crafting components while leveling?
faqs.answer: Keep materials linked to your active professions and near-term recipes, then clear excess components that do not feed current goals.

faqs.question: When should I keep gems and jewelry instead of selling them?
faqs.answer: Save cuts and attuned gems that align with your planned stat path, especially when they support gear you will keep for multiple tiers.

faqs.question: Are mounts and gliders always worth keeping?
faqs.answer: Yes. Treat them as collection and traversal rewards because many are rare drops or guaranteed vault unlocks.

faqs.question: How do I spend currencies and upgrade items efficiently?
faqs.answer: Invest gold and upgrade resources only in items that support your current class setup, profession lane, or immediate progression target.

faqs.question: Do job tools matter if they are not combat gear?
faqs.answer: They do. Gathering and crafting tools unlock the loops that supply your gear, consumables, and upgrade materials.

lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.title: Farever Mounts Pets and Gliders
lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.intro: Farever includes broad collection systems for mounts, companions, gliders, and cosmetic rewards. These systems support mobility, exploration routing, and long-term collection goals during Early Access.

faqs.question: What does the launch mount collection include?
faqs.answer: Launch examples include wolf, deer, skunk, crab, and ladybug variants. Mounts are practical travel tools and collection goals, with item listings tracking movement speed multipliers.

faqs.question: How important are gliders in Farever?
faqs.answer: Gliders are central to open-world movement. They support cliff traversal, ravine routes, cave exits, and vertical exploration paths where direct ground travel is slower.

faqs.question: What companions are available at launch?
faqs.answer: Companion-style collectibles include frogs, grassflopper, buttontail, and tortrock examples. They are identity and collection rewards rather than direct combat power upgrades.

faqs.question: Can mounts and gliders drop from creature loot tables?
faqs.answer: Yes. Some creature drops include rare mount or glider rewards with low drop rates, such as 0.10% reward entries, making repeated farming routes valuable for collectors.

faqs.question: Do vault runs provide guaranteed collection rewards?
faqs.answer: Several vault reward pools include guaranteed cosmetic collection unlocks like specific gliders or mounts, often paired with gold rewards, so vault routing is a consistent collection method.

faqs.question: How does the optional cosmetic shop fit into progression?
faqs.answer: Shop visuals are optional. Planned cosmetic offerings focus on appearance customization for armor skins, mounts, companions, and gliders without replacing core progression loops.

faqs.question: What collection priority is best early on?
faqs.answer: Prioritize movement value first with mounts and gliders that improve daily route speed, then expand into rare drops and long-horizon vanity collection goals.

lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.title: Farever Solo and Co-op Multiplayer Guide
lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.intro: Farever supports both solo play and 4-player co-op parties. The same core loop works across formats: explore, clear activities, run instances, defeat bosses, and progress your build.

settings.name: Solo-friendly exploration and progression
settings.description: Solo players can progress smoothly through regions, activities, crafting loops, and build upgrades while choosing their own pace and routing priorities.

settings.name: 4-player party structure
settings.description: Party groups can include up to four players, with role splitting across damage, sustain, control, and frontline pressure for faster and safer clears.

settings.name: Shared loot, XP, and party rewards
settings.description: Co-op progression supports shared loot and XP flows, post-fight distribution, party trading windows, and shared completion value in repeatable content.

settings.name: MMO-style world interaction
settings.description: Public areas, world layers, chat channels, and region hubs allow players to meet others while exploring without forcing permanent party grouping.

settings.name: Regional server and friend setup
settings.description: Choose the closest region before inviting friends to minimize latency. Current launch regions include Europe, North America, Latin America, Asia, and Mainland China.

settings.name: Scalable activity difficulty
settings.description: Several activity routes scale by group size so players can approach world objectives solo or with a team and still maintain progress efficiency.

lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.title: Farever Roadmap and Upcoming Content
lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.intro: Farever launched with a strong Early Access baseline and a roadmap centered on world growth, deeper classes, more instance challenge layers, social systems, and long-term progression.

entries.title: Early Access launch baseline
entries.description: Launch includes four classes, level 20 progression, two regions, ten instance encounters, six jobs, 100+ weapon skills, 150+ gear pieces, and 100+ collection elements.

entries.title: World expansion phase
entries.description: Planned updates add new regions such as Peak of Obral and City of Ramburg, plus more factions, dungeons, bosses, world events, and activity types.

entries.title: Instance progression updates
entries.description: Roadmap targets include Heroic Mode, max-level instance modes, additional boss mechanics, challenge modifiers, and more unique completion rewards.

entries.title: Character growth expansion
entries.description: Progression direction includes raising the level cap toward 50, adding classes like Druid and Monk, and introducing deeper talent trees and class skill systems.

entries.title: Collections and job depth
entries.description: Future content plans include fishing, archaeology, more mounts, gliders, critters, toys, and major increases in unique weapons, skills, and gear pieces.

entries.title: Social and PvP systems
entries.description: Upcoming systems include guilds, auction house support, friends tools, duels, PvP regions, instanced battlegrounds, and party quality-of-life features.

entries.title: 1.0 release direction
entries.description: The full release path aims to extend world scale, class variety, instance challenge, seasonal activities, and long-term social progression systems.

lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.title: Farever System Requirements and Performance Guide
lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.intro: Use this checklist to match official hardware targets, choose the right server region, and keep stable 1080p performance during Early Access sessions.

steps.title: Confirm operating system and storage baseline
steps.description: Farever requires a 64-bit Windows setup and at least 25 GB free storage. Keep additional free space for patch downloads and update extraction.

steps.title: Check CPU, RAM, and GPU tier fit
steps.description: Minimum targets are around i7 6700K or Ryzen 5 5500, 16 GB RAM, and RTX 3060 or RX 5600 XT. Recommended targets move to 32 GB RAM with RTX 4060 or RX 7600 class GPUs.

steps.title: Use the official FPS expectations
steps.description: Minimum profile targets 1080p at 30 FPS and recommended profile targets 1080p at 60 FPS. Tune quality settings according to these baseline expectations.

steps.title: Choose the nearest server region
steps.description: Available regions include Europe, North America, Latin America, Asia, and Mainland China. Picking the closest region improves latency and party responsiveness.

steps.title: Apply a repeatable performance checklist
steps.description: If frame pacing drops, lower the highest-cost graphics options first, close background workloads, retest in a fixed combat route, and adjust one setting group at a time.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Key answers for launch, progression, and multiplayer planning.

questions.question: Is Farever available now?
questions.answer: Yes, it is available on Steam Early Access for PC.

questions.question: What class should first-time players start with?
questions.answer: Warrior is the most forgiving opening choice, while Cleric is excellent for group-focused starts.

questions.question: Can I play solo and still progress smoothly?
questions.answer: Yes. Solo progression works well when you keep upgrades targeted and route your activities efficiently.

questions.question: How important is crafting early?
questions.answer: Very important. Jobs and recipes create steady power growth between dungeon attempts.

questions.question: What should I focus on after the first dungeon clears?
questions.answer: Refine one build path, improve weapon synergy, and use loot routing to unlock your next upgrade threshold.

questions.question: How can I keep performance stable?
questions.answer: Keep drivers updated, tune high-impact graphics settings first, and use the nearest server region for lower latency.

whySteps.step1Description: Track daily and weekly movement to understand whether activity is rising, stable, or cooling.
whySteps.step2Description: Use peak-hour patterns to schedule co-op content when party formation is easier.
whySteps.step3Description: Compare trend changes before and after updates to spot which releases shift player behavior.
